breaking.title: Iran holds funeral for Ali Khamenei: Who is attending?
breaking.summary: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28, 2026, in joint precision strikes by the US and Israeli militaries. The airstrikes killed Khamenei as well as other members of his family.

breaking.title: Jantar Mantar turns hunger strike junction
breaking.summary: The triangle of strikers have a common demand, the resignation of the Union Education Minister; activist Sonam Wangchuk is on the CJP stage; six CPI(ML) students and nine unaffiliated protesters sit on either side

breaking.title: 'Under attack by unknown assailants': British military reports fresh cargo ship attack off Yemen
breaking.summary: Off the coast of Hodeidah, Yemen, a cargo ship was attacked by unidentified armed forces, prompting an urgent investigation. This alarming event is set against a backdrop of heightened threats from Houthi rebels, who have been known to target commercial shipping. Concurrently, ground battles between government forces and Houthis have escalated, leading to numerous casualties, adding to the region's maritime security concerns, which also encompass ongoing piracy activities.

breaking.title: ‘People can be killed but ideals can't’: Iran condemns Trump's 'one shot' threat
breaking.summary: Iran has fiercely condemned US President Donald Trump's remarks about potentially eliminating its leadership, accusing America of lacking "civilisation, history, or honour." Trump's comments, made in an interview, questioned the sincerity of mourners at the funeral of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and suggested a swift strike could eliminate Iran's top officials. The funeral itself has drawn massive crowds, projected by Iran as a display of national unity and defiance.

breaking.title: Shooting in US on fourth of July: 8, including 4 children, injured in Brooklyn's Coney Island
breaking.summary: Tragedy struck Coney Island on Friday night as a shooting left at least eight people injured, including four young children. The incident occurred around 10:37 pm, with victims ranging from six to 37 years old. While most are in stable condition, a 21-year-old woman is critically injured. Police have recovered a firearm but no arrests have been made as investigations continue into the disturbing event.

breaking.title: Karachi shopping centre fire: 11-year-old named accused in chargesheet over blaze that killed 72
breaking.summary: A tragic fire at Karachi's Gul Plaza shopping centre, claiming 72 lives, has seen an 11-year-old boy named as an accused. Police allege he was playing with matchsticks when the blaze began. His father and four management committee members also face trial for negligence and failing to enforce safety measures. The building reportedly lacked crucial fire safety systems, exacerbating the disaster.

breaking.title: V. Sivankutty urges Kerala government to resolve administrative paralysis at Thiruvananthapuram Corporation
breaking.summary: Sivankutty alleges that attempts by NDA-led Corporation’s leadership to protect Vazhottukonam ward councillor R. Sugathan, who is under judicial remand after being arrested under provisions of Kerala Anti-Social Activities Prevention Act, has disrupted day-to-day functioning of the local body

breaking.title: Gwadar Explained: Why Pak's Chinese-Backed Port Keeps Coming Under Attack
breaking.summary: China developed Gwadar Port as a strategic and commercial hub under its Belt and Road Initiative. (File)
